compiled_state.title: [worktree isolation]: sub-agent dispatched with isolation:worktree -> agent worktree persists after agent completion, accumulating over sessions
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When a sub-agent is dispatched with \`isolation: worktree\`, the runtime creates a temporary worktree under \`.claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/\`. Per the Agent tool documentation: "the worktree is automatically cleaned up if the agent makes no changes; otherwise the path and branch are returned in the result." Once the orchestrator has harvested the agent's work (via \`harvest-worktree.sh\` or equivalent), the worktree should be removed so it does not persist indefinitely.

#### Actual Behavior

Agent worktrees persist in \`.claude/worktrees/\` after agent completion and across sessions, accumulating over time. Current \`git worktree list\` output from this repo shows 22 \`agent-*\` worktrees still registered, the majority marked \`locked\`:

\`\`\`
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a185b801   e403dd9a [worktree-agent-a185b801] locked
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1f52ce4   a9df56e8 [worktree-agent-a1f52ce4] locked
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2137f12   21ed4767 [worktree-agent-a2137f12] locked
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2a466ac   2cc2cdfb [worktree-agent-a2a466ac] locked
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a3a8d0f4   05e3464a [worktree-agent-a3a8d0f4] locked
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a586392c   65986cfb [worktree-agent-a586392c] locked
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a6b94240   c2a45e10 [worktree-agent-a6b94240]
/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a6c9afb1   6d401c32 [worktree-agent-a6c9afb1] locked
... (22 total agent-* worktrees, 21 locked, 1 unlocked)
\`\`\`

Each entry has an associated \`worktree-agent-<id>\` branch still registered. The count grows across sessions — there is no automatic pruning. User flagged this as "gradually increasing number of orphan worktrees in the environment."

### 3. Technical Environment

* Platform: darwin 25.4.0
* Current branch: worktree-20260416-163115
* Relevant docs: \`plugins/dso/docs/WORKTREE-GUIDE.md\`, \`plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/worktree-dispatch.md\`
* Harvest script: \`plugins/dso/scripts/harvest-worktree.sh\`

### 4. Action History

* Observation only. Ran \`git worktree list\` in the repo; 22 \`agent-*\` worktrees were present, 21 locked.
* No cleanup attempted yet.

### 5. Investigation Pointers (non-speculative)

Workflows to review for worktree lifecycle:
* \`/dso:sprint\`, \`/dso:fix-bug\`, \`/dso:debug-everything\` — dispatch sub-agents with \`isolation: worktree\`
* \`harvest-worktree.sh\` — harvests agent work into session branch
* \`claude-safe\` worktree cleanup (CLAUDE.md "Worktree lifecycle" section) — documents cleanup for session worktrees (\`worktree-<timestamp>/\`), not clear whether agent-\* worktrees are covered

The \`locked\` flag on most entries suggests either:
(a) the lock is set during agent execution and never released on normal completion, or
(b) the lock is released but the worktree + branch are not removed afterward.

comments.body:
Consolidating 049c-df32 (duplicate) into this ticket. Additional signals from the 2026-04-16 evidence session:
- 21 agent-* worktrees present, all locked; 6 had uncommitted scratch files; 1 had an unmerged RED-test commit.
- 82 worktree-agent-* branches remained after manual worktree removal — branch cleanup is fully orphaned from worktree cleanup.
- Of the 82 branches, 75 were ancestors of main (safe merged) and 7 were unmerged; all 7 referenced tickets (3601-f085, 615f-fad3, 639d-fe1b, 6b3b-41b0) that were already closed, i.e. work was superseded via other paths.
- User-supplied correlation: the sessions that produced these worktrees had auto-compact events. Recorded as evidence, not cause.

Known-issue match: none in KNOWN-ISSUES.md.
Hypothesis surface for investigation:
(a) claude-safe _offer_worktree_cleanup scope: does it cover .claude/worktrees/agent-* or only the session worktree path?
(b) worktree-cleanup.sh scope: does it traverse agent-* worktrees, and does it delete the backing branch alongside the worktree?
(c) harvest-worktree.sh post-harvest action: after successful harvest, does it remove the agent worktree or leave it locked?
(d) auto-compact interaction: whether auto-compact drops the in-progress orchestrator state that would trigger post-harvest cleanup.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
BASIC investigation complete (confidence: high, 8/8 hypotheses confirmed).

ROOT CAUSE: No script-level lifecycle hook removes an agent worktree or deletes its backing worktree-agent-<hex> branch after harvest-worktree.sh succeeds. Cleanup is specified only in the orchestrator-executed prompt skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md Step 7. fix-bug/SKILL.md dispatches with isolation:worktree but never reads per-worktree-review-commit.md and never calls harvest-worktree.sh (the Step 7 references in fix-bug are the 'Verify Fix' step, not worktree cleanup). debug-everything likewise lacks a cleanup step. harvest-worktree.sh ends at 'merged successfully' with zero worktree/branch cleanup calls.

Hypotheses confirmed:
1. worktree-agent-<hex> naming is assigned by the Claude Code harness when isolation:worktree is passed; no DSO script creates or manages this pattern.
2. harvest-worktree.sh performs merge only — no cleanup after success.
3. per-worktree-review-commit.md Step 7 is the only place post-harvest cleanup is specified.
4. fix-bug/SKILL.md never references per-worktree-review-commit.md or harvest-worktree.sh.
5. claude-safe._offer_worktree_cleanup targets only the session worktree (~/<repo>-worktrees/worktree-YYYYMMDD), never agent worktrees.
6. worktree-cleanup.sh does enumerate agent-* worktrees and the orphan-branch pattern worktree-* does match worktree-agent-*, but is blocked by the 12h age gate and requires explicit user invocation.
7. Agent-worktree locked markers (e.g. '.git/worktrees/agent-*/locked') contain harness PIDs that persist after sub-agent exit; no DSO script calls git worktree unlock.
8. per-worktree-review-commit.md Steps 5 (harvest) and 7 (cleanup) are multiple orchestrator turns apart — an auto-compact between them drops the cleanup intent, matching the user-reported correlation.

Proposed fixes (awaiting user approval):
1. Extend harvest-worktree.sh to do unconditional post-merge cleanup (unlock + worktree remove --force + prune + branch -D). Risk: low. Removes LLM-in-loop dependency entirely.
2. Add agent-branch reclamation to worktree-cleanup.sh as defense-in-depth (skip 12h gate for agent-* worktrees, unlock before remove, ensure pattern match). Risk: low.
3. Add cleanup block to fix-bug/debug-everything SKILL.md (or factor shared prompt). Risk: medium — preserves LLM-in-loop failure mode, so best only as complement to Fix 1.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Fix verified GREEN.
- Fix 1 (harvest-worktree.sh post-merge cleanup): 16/16 tests pass (4 new: cleanup_removes_worktree, cleanup_deletes_backing_branch, cleanup_skipped_on_gate_failure, cleanup_handles_missing_worktree_gracefully).
- Fix 2 (worktree-cleanup.sh agent reclamation): 9/9 tests pass in new tests/scripts/test-worktree-cleanup.sh (agent_worktree_exempt_from_age_gate, non_agent_worktree_respects_age_gate, is_old_enough_returns_true_for_agent_path, explicit_unlock_before_remove_does_not_error + regression guards).

Gates:
- Gate 2a (reversal): triggered:false (intent-aligned suppressed).
- Gate 2c (test regression): triggered:false (intent-aligned suppressed).
- Gate 2d (dependency): triggered:false (no new deps).
- Scope-drift (dso:dso:scope-drift-reviewer): triggered:false, drift_classification:in_scope, confidence:high.

Anti-pattern scan: the conceptual anti-pattern is 'dispatches with isolation:worktree but never harvests' — this recurs in fix-bug/Step 2 (investigation), fix-bug/Step 6 (fix impl), and debug-everything dispatch points. These are covered by epic c737-977d (placeholder for the fix-bug/debug-everything integration gap) and out of scope for this commit. No code-level anti-pattern occurrences within Fix 1/Fix 2 semantics.
comments.author: Test
